explanation:
Personal finance and investment use universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies standard financial industry formulas.

## How the Calculation Works

Identify arbitrage opportunities by comparing interest rates between different banks.

This calculator applies the formula using your inputs, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unusually high or low values.

## Benchmark Returns by Time Horizon

| Time Horizon | S&P 500 | 10-Year Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4–5% | 3–5% |
| 5 years | ~8% average | ~4% | Inflation rate |
| 10 years | ~9–10% | ~4% | Below inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Core Investment Principles

- **Stay the course**: Don't abandon your plan during market downturns. Emotion is your worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: Spread investments across different assets, sectors, and geographies.
- **Keep costs low**: A 1%+ annual fee reduces your 30-year returns by ~30%.
- **Think long-term**: Stock investments work over 10+ years, not in 1-year windows.
- **Rebalance annually**: Adjust portfolio allocations once per year, no more.

## Important Disclaimers

This calculation is a reference tool only. For critical financial, medical, or legal decisions, consult a qualified professional. Rates are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: Taking advantage of interest rate differences between banks or institutions to earn additional returns on your deposits with minimal risk.

faq.q: Is it legal to move money between banks for better rates?
faq.a: Yes, completely legal. You're simply choosing where to place your funds to earn better returns.

faq.q: When doesn't interest rate arbitrage make sense?
faq.a: When wire fees, transfer costs, or account opening fees exceed the interest rate spread you'd earn.

faq.q: How do I compare rates between banks?
faq.a: Use online banking comparison tools, visit bank websites directly, or use this calculator to compare APY across institutions.
Counterparty risk is minimal in the US—FDIC insurance covers up to $250,000 per bank per depositor.

faq.q: How long does it take to open a new account?
faq.a: Online account opening typically takes 24–48 hours, with immediate access to funds.

faq.q: What deposit minimums should I expect?
faq.a: Varies by bank. Many offer no-minimum online savings accounts; others require $1,000+ for premium rates.

faq.q: What average returns should I expect from the stock market?
faq.a: Historically, the S&P 500 returns ~10% nominal annually (7% real, adjusted for inflation). Past performance doesn't guarantee future results, but it's a useful benchmark.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Formula is fixed and doesn't require external data updates
